findings.summary: certification passed when all continuity evidence is certified

explanations.summary: certification exists to certify end-to-end planning evidence continuity deterministically
the certification scope includes graph, governance, compatibility, evaluation, session, scenario, aggregation, and integrity evidence
aggregation evidence was checked for planning continuity certification
compatibility evidence was checked for planning continuity certification
evaluation evidence was checked for planning continuity certification
governance evidence was checked for planning continuity certification
graph_foundations evidence was checked for planning continuity certification
integrity evidence was checked for planning continuity certification
scenario evidence was checked for planning continuity certification
session evidence was checked for planning continuity certification
continuity guarantees passed when hashes and scope evidence remained stable
failed or uncertified guarantees remain fail-visible
provenance was certified through scope and evidence lineage
replay and rollback certification evidence remains non-executable
integrity enforcement was certified as part of the continuity scope
execution-boundary certification passed only because no execution readiness, authorization, routing, scheduling, dispatch, traversal, optimization, or recommendation signal exists
certified continuity does not authorize execution and does not mark runtime execution readiness

explicit_limitations: certified continuity does not authorize execution
certification does not mark runtime execution readiness
certification does not route, schedule, dispatch, traverse, optimize, recommend, or execute
certification evidence is planning-readiness evidence only

finding_records.summary: certification passed when all continuity evidence is certified

findings.message: hidden risk state detection remains fail-visible
